title: Public Health Agency of Canada's Forward Regulatory Plan 2026 to 2028: Amending the Schedule to the Quarantine Act - Canada.ca
content:
## Public Health Agency of Canada's Forward Regulatory Plan 2026 to 2028: Amending the Schedule to the Quarantine Act

## Title of the regulatory initiative
Amending the Schedule to the Quarantine Act
We are proposing an administrative update to the Schedule to the Quarantine Act that will group individually listed diseases into broader, more flexible categories. The changes would reduce the need for frequent legislative amendments when new diseases emerge or existing disease classifications change.
The proposed approach will maintain strong public health protections while reducing unnecessary administrative burden.
This legislative initiative is associated with the Public Health Agency of Canada's Regulatory Stock Review Plan .
We have also identified this legislative initiative in the Health Canada and the Public Health Agency of Canada’s report on Red Tape Review.

## Regulatory cooperation efforts (domestic and international)
This initiative is not part of a formal regulatory cooperation work plan.
The proposed amendments to the Schedule will make it easier to address health threats by reducing the need to update the law as often. This will reduce the Government of Canada’s administrative burden and facilitate efficient response to communicable disease threats.
Stakeholders that may be impacted by the proposal include other Government of Canada departments and agencies involved in the administration of the Quarantine Act ,as well as travellers and conveyance operators (including operators of conveyances in the air, marine, road, and rail modes) arriving in, or in the process of departing Canada.
Approaches to consultation, including opportunities for people in Canada to provide input, will be determined as the proposal progresses.
- Consulting with Canadians
- List of acts and regulations
- Regulatory cooperation in Canada
- Developing and improving federal regulations

## Departmental contact information
Janice Merhej Director, Policy, Programs and Travel Health Centre for Border and Travel Health Regulatory, Operations and Emergency Management Branch Public Health Agency of Canada Email: janice.merhej@phac-aspc.gc.ca

## Date the regulatory initiative was first included in the Forward Regulatory Plan

## Stakeholder / Media sensitivities
No stakeholder or media sensitivities are anticipated, as the proposed amendments are administrative in nature and do not introduce new authorities, requirements, or impacts on individuals or industry. The update is intended to streamline and modernize the Schedule to the Quarantine Act, without changing how the Act is applied.
